title: Shared Cross-Platform Test Definition
description: Defines acceptance test cases that must pass identically on all Bot API platforms (Java, C#, Python, TypeScript).

suite.description: Unique suite identifier matching the file name (e.g., 'intent-validation').

description.description: Human-readable summary of what this suite covers.

id.description: Acceptance ID from TEST-REGISTRY.md. Append a short suffix for the specific scenario (e.g., 'TR-API-CMD-002-fire-valid').

description.description: What this test case verifies. Include whether it is a positive or negative test.

type.description: Test kind: 'positive' (happy path), 'negative' (rejection/edge), 'scenario' (stateful step-sequence), or 'botDefault' (BaseBot accessor default-value contract).

method.description: The API method under test, using the Java name (e.g., 'setFire', 'setTurnRate'). Required for positive/negative tests; unused for scenario tests.

setup.description: Pre-conditions to establish before calling the method. Keys are state field names (e.g., 'energy', 'gunHeat', 'maxTurnRate'). Platform runners translate to local setup.

args.description: Positional arguments to pass to the method. Use the string 'NaN' for IEEE NaN, 'Infinity'/'-Infinity' for infinities, and null for absent/null arguments.

expected.description: Expected outcome for positive/negative tests. At least one of 'returns', field assertions, or 'throws' must be present.

returns.description: Expected return value from the method call. Use true/false for booleans, numbers for numeric returns.

throws.description: Expected exception type using the Java name. Platform runners map to local exception names (e.g., 'IllegalArgumentException' → 'ArgumentException' in C#, 'ValueError' in Python).

returnsEmpty.description: When true, the method must return an empty collection (e.g., getBulletStates() → empty Set/List).

additionalProperties.description: Additional keys are state field assertions checked after the method call (e.g., 'firepower': 1.5, 'turnRate': 10.0).

steps.description: Ordered sequence of operations for scenario tests. Each step is either an addEvent or dispatchEvents action.

eventType.description: Event class simple name for addEvent (e.g., 'WonRoundEvent', 'DeathEvent').

turnNumber.description: Turn number stamped on the event (for addEvent).

repeat.description: How many times to repeat this step. Defaults to 1.

atTurn.description: Current turn number argument passed to dispatchEvents.

expectAfter.description: Expected outcome after all steps have been executed.

dispatchOrder.description: Expected sequence of dispatched event type names (simple class names) in order.

queueSize.description: Expected number of events remaining in the queue after all steps.
